Reentrancy in TimelockExecutor.executeBatch(uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#52-61):
	External calls:
	- pc.target.call{value: pc.value}(pc.data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#57)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- pc.executed = true (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#56)
	TimelockExecutor.pendingCalls (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#18) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- TimelockExecutor.cancelCall(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#63-66)
	- TimelockExecutor.executeBatch(uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#52-61)
	- TimelockExecutor.executeCall(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#41-50)
	- TimelockExecutor.getPendingCount() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#73-75)
	- TimelockExecutor.pendingCalls (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#18)
	- TimelockExecutor.scheduleCall(address,bytes,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#34-39)

Reentrancy in TimelockExecutor.executeCall(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#41-50):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = pc.target.call{value: pc.value}(pc.data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#47)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- CallExecuted(id,pc.target) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#49)

Reentrancy in TimelockExecutor.executeBatch(uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#52-61):
	External calls:
	- pc.target.call{value: pc.value}(pc.data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#57)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- CallExecuted(ids[i],pc.target) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#58)

TimelockExecutor.cancelCall(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#63-66) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(id < pendingCalls.length,invalid id) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#64)

TimelockExecutor.executeCall(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#41-50) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(id < pendingCalls.length,invalid id) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#42)
	- require(bool,string)(! pc.executed,already executed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#44)
	- require(bool,string)(block.timestamp >= pc.readyAt,timelock active) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#45)

TimelockExecutor.executeBatch(uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#52-61) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- ! pc.executed && block.timestamp >= pc.readyAt (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#55)

TimelockExecutor.executeBatch(uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#52-61) ignores return value by pc.target.call{value: pc.value}(pc.data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_e_gs1jud.sol#57)
Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.

The return value of the low-level call is not checked, so if the call fails, the Ether will be locked in the contract. If the low level is used to prevent blocking operations, consider logging failed calls. Ensure that the return value of a low-level call is checked or logged.
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.
Invoking `SSTORE` operations in loops may waste gas. Use a local variable to hold the loop computation result.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
State variables that are only changed in the constructor should be declared immutable to save gas. Add the `immutable` attribute to state variables that are only changed in the constructor
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`
